_meta.doc: Sidecar metadata for data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json. The whitelist itself is a flat top-level JSON array of 106 single-kanji strings — that shape is intentional because furigana.js, audit_dokkai_kanji_scope.py, audit_teacher_review.py, and check_content_integrity.py all parse it as `set(json.loads(...))`. Adding inline metadata would break those callers, so docs live here.
_meta.source: Curated from KnowledgeBank/kanji_n5.md by tools/build_data.py. The list mirrors the official JEES JLPT N5 kanji syllabus.
_meta.ordering_convention: Author-curated (roughly first-encountered-in-lesson-order, not strictly sorted by stroke count or Unicode). The order is stable across builds; new additions go at the end.
_meta.exceptions: data/dokkai_kanji_exception.json lists kanji that may appear in dokkai passages and (since 2026-05-08) in moji/goi/bunpou paper distractors despite NOT being in the whitelist. JA-13 + JA-28 + JA-40 enforce the surface-specific rules.
see_also: data/kanji.json (full per-kanji records with examples + stroke-order SVG paths)

known_gaps_vs_full_n5_syllabus.doc: Kanji that appear in mainstream N5 reference sources (JEES official sample, Genki I, Minna no Nihongo I, Try! N5, Shin Kanzen Master N5, So-matome N5) but are NOT currently in this whitelist. Surfaced during the 2026-05-14 audit (ISSUE-002). The whitelist is intentionally capped at 106 to match the existing content-authoring envelope: each kanji in the whitelist has a full record in kanji.json (readings, mnemonic, look-alike clusters, examples) AND a stroke-order SVG under svg/kanji/ AND linked vocab entries in vocab.json. Adding new kanji requires authoring all of those artifacts; this list documents the queue without mis-classifying these kanji as N4 (they are NOT N4 — they are N5 in mainstream syllabi).

queued_n5_kanji.rationale: Genki I L8, MnN L5, Try! N5; appears in 多い (many) — basic adjective
Genki I L8, MnN L5; appears in 少ない (few), 少し (a little)
Genki I L3, MnN L5; 帰る (return home) — daily-routine verb
Genki I L8, MnN L4; 早い (early/fast) — basic adjective, distinct from 速い (also fast — N4)
Genki I L7, MnN L6; 物 (thing), 食べ物 (food), 飲み物 (drink)
Borderline N5/N4; in MnN L6 vocab but JEES sample uses kana. Conservative defer until next content pass.

known_gaps_vs_full_n5_syllabus.remediation_owner: future content-authoring pass — full kanji.json entry + svg/kanji/<glyph>.svg KanjiVG stroke order + ≥2 vocab.json entries linking the kanji form. Estimated effort: ~1 hour per kanji including SVG sourcing under CC-BY-SA 3.0.
